center.name: 喬治·紐博爾德·勞倫斯
center.summary: 喬治·紐博爾德·勞倫斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联13种鸟类，包括Antillean nighthawk、Cinnamon becard、Black-tailed myiobius等13种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Antillean nighthawk (Chordeiles gundlachii) is a nightjar native to the Caribbean, The Bahamas, and Florida Keys

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-tailed myiobius or black-tailed flycatcher (Myiobius atricaudus) is a species of passerine bird in the family Onychorhynchidae. It was previously placed in family Tyrannidae or Tityridae. Black-tailed flycatchers are found in Brazil, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Panama, Peru, and Venezuela. Their natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and heavily degraded former forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Carmiol's tanager (Chlorothraupis carmioli) is a species of bird in the cardinal family Cardinalidae that is found in Central America from Nicaragua southwards to northwest Colombia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The cinnamon becard (Pachyramphus cinnamomeus) is a passerine bird in the family Tityridae, the tityras, becards, and allies. It is found in Mexico, in every Central American country except El Salvador, and in Colombia, Ecuador, and Venezuela.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: obscurior, Ecuador | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Myiozetetes | species = granadensis | authority = Lawrence, 1862 | range_map = Myiozetetes granadensis map.svg }} The grey-capped flycatcher (Myiozetetes granadensis) is a passerine bird, a member of the large tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Honduras, Nicaragua, Panama, Peru, and Venezuela.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Hauxwell's thrush (Turdus hauxwelli) is a species of bird in the family Turdidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and possibly Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: LeConte's thrasher (Toxostoma lecontei) is a pale bird found in the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The lesser elaenia (Elaenia chiriquensis) is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Costa Rica, Panama, on Trinidad, in every mainland South American country except Chile and Uruguay, and as a vagrant on Bonaire and Curaçao.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Panama flycatcher (Myiarchus panamensis) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Panama, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The scarlet-thighed dacnis (Dacnis venusta) is a tanager native to Central and northernmost South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The tawny-rumped tyrannulet (Tyranniscus uropygialis) is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Venezuela, and possibly Argentina.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Venezuelan flycatcher (Myiarchus venezuelensis) is a passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia, Venezuela, and on Tobago.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Lanyon|Lanyon]], 1984 | species = inornata | authority = (Lawrence, 1869) | synonyms = * Myiozetetes inornatus (protonym) * Conopias inornatus | range_map = Phelpsia inornata map.svg }} The white-bearded flycatcher (Phelpsia inornata) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Colombia and Venezuela.Remsen, J
